title: Classical motor imagery dataset with left hand, right hand, and rest
description: A classical motor imagery EEG dataset comprising recordings from 7 healthy subjects performing left hand, right hand, and passive rest tasks. The dataset contains 19-channel EEG data sampled at 200 Hz with visual cue-based motor imagery paradigm, designed for brain-computer interface research and benchmarking. This derivative dataset is organized in BIDS format and includes standardized HED event annotations for reproducible analysis.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Nihon Kohden EEG-1200 system with 19 channels positioned according to the standard 10-20 montage (Fp1, Fp2, F3, F4, C3, C4, P3, P4, O1, O2, F7, F8, T3, T4, T5, T6, Fz, Cz, Pz). Sampling rate was 200 Hz with reference computed as 0.55*(C3+C4) and ground at earlobes (A1, A2). Subjects performed motor imagery tasks cued by visual arrow stimuli with 1-second trial duration and 1.5-2.5 second inter-trial intervals. The paradigm included three conditions: left hand imagery, right hand imagery, and passive rest.
